meanings.definition: the alliance of nations that fought the Axis in World War II and which (with subsequent additions) signed the charter of the United Nations in 1945
in World War I the alliance of Great Britain and France and Russia and all the other nations that became allied with them in opposing the Central Powers
an alliance of nations joining together to fight a common enemy
an associate who provides cooperation or assistance
examples: he's a good ally in fight

meanings.definition: become an ally or associate, as by a treaty or marriage
examples: He allied himself with the Communists
